body_txt: This is a set-up where Alice reappears after a long absence disguised and/or changed by the passage of time and then reveals herself to Bob and other characters. Typically, she will be unrecognizable until she tells Bob to look closely, at which point her identity will be obvious. Whether Bob's reaction is pleased or horrified will depend on his relationship with Alice, although this trope is notably present in fiction dealing with revenge , particularly in plots where Alice Came Back with a Vengeance . One specific case of this is Old Friend, New Gender . Compare Something Only They Would Say . Contrast Did You Get a New Haircut? , which is when a character is still recognized despite the drastic change in appearance. When removing a mask, it overlaps with a Dramatic Unmask . When the person who's changed is usually still recognisable, they're on the receiving end of an "All Grown Up" Remark . Unrelated to any changes No Doubt has made over the years.
